# Careers
Unique technology. Outstanding people. Let’s build the future of Quantum computing together.
## Working at Nord Quantique
At Nord Quantique we’re working to change the world through quantum computing, and that means working in a highly dynamic environment at the forefront of quantum technology development, at the heart of Sherbrooke’s vibrant Quantum Innovation Zone ecosystem. Our interdisciplinary team is laser-focused on solving grand challenges and making quantum computing a reality. 
Nord Quantique is an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive work environment where all individuals can fulfill their aspirations while being supported and heard. This also means that we offer flexible work schedule and adapt our methods for the wellbeing of every individual. 
## Coming home to Nord Quantique 
Nord Quantique offers extensive moving support, including help with immigration procedures, finding a suitable home, access to childcare and schools, even helping your partner to find a new job. We offer a relocation compensation package and competitive salaries. 
Quantum System Validation Team Lead 
**Quantum System Validation Team Lead****  
**(permanent position, 40 hours per week)  
**Contact:**(819) 975-4654**,** career@nordquantique.ca
**About**[**Nord Quantique**](http://www.nordquantique.ca/)
At Nord Quantique, we strive to build impactful technology by relentlessly pushing our understanding of quantum science. We are an interdisciplinary team of scientists, engineers, innovators, builders, and enthusiasts on a mission to make quantum computing a reality. Our quantum processors are designed to reduce errors in quantum computation to facilitate long-term scaling toward fully-fledged quantum computers. We believe it is our responsibility to deliver this transformative technology for the greater good of society.
Located at the heart of the beautiful Eastern Townships region in the province of Quebec, Canada, we develop our technology in the vibrant quantum ecosystem of Sherbrooke. Leaning on more than a decade of vertical integration of quantum hardware, we are leveraging a comprehensive pool of quantum technology and micro-electronic infrastructure to deploy our quantum computing technology.
**Our current needs**
We are seeking a senior technical leader with strong expertise in engineering of quantum systems with an emphasis on large-scale experimental validation and reliability engineering. Beyond technical excellence, we are looking for someone capable of building and leading a multidisciplinary validation organization operating at the interface between R&D and large-scale system deployment.
The mission of the Quantum System Validation team is to validate preservation of multimode bosonic logical performance under scaling-induced physical variability and emergent error channels, and to enforce the reliability standards required for deployment-ready quantum computing systems.
More specifically, this individual will define and grow Nord Quantique’s system-level validation strategy, establish reliability engineering discipline across increasingly complex quantum computing systems, and lead the development of scalable validation methodologies supporting future utility-scale architectures.
The role combines quantum system validation, operational robustness engineering, and long-duration reliability qualification of a scaled and integrated system covering cryogenic, control, and software.
**Keys responsibilities:**
  * Define and lead the system-level validation and reliability strategy across successive quantum computer generations
  * Build and support a multidisciplinary validation organization spanning quantum validation, operational robustness, and reliability engineering
  * Define deployment-readiness criteria, quantitative system acceptance metrics, performance gates, and validation methodologies supporting deployment decisions
  * Lead long-duration stability testing and drift characterization campaigns
  * Establish FMEA methodologies, qualification procedures, and reliability governance frameworks
  * Validate preservation of logical performance under scaling-induced physical variability and emergent error channels
  * Oversee validation of hardware–software orchestration, including calibration workflows, boot-up sequencing, and software integration
  * Coordinate cross-functional root-cause analysis activities during system bring-up and validation failures


**Education and experience requirements:**
  * PhD in experimental physics, engineering, quantum computing, systems engineering, or a related field
  * +5 years of postgraduate experience in quantum technologies, advanced hardware systems, or complex cryogenic system development
  * Including 2 years of relevant industry experience
  * Demonstrated experience leading large-scale experimental or system validation efforts
  * Experience building or managing multidisciplinary technical teams
  * Strong experience operating at the interface between research and engineering environments


We recognize that expertise can come from a variety of academic or professional paths—not all of which follow conventional trajectories—and we value diversity in that regard.
**Sought expertise and knowledge:**
  * Superconducting quantum computing systems 
  * System-level validation and performance qualification 
  * Scaling-induced noise and drift mechanisms 
  * Reliability engineering and FMEA methodologies 
  * Long-duration stability testing 
  * Hardware–software orchestration validation 
  * Statistical analysis of complex experimental systems 
  * Cross-functional technical leadership 
  * Advanced level in English, to communicate with international team members 
  * Intermediate knowledge of French, an asset


**Sought profile:**
  * Strong engineering and systems-thinking mindset 
  * Ability to build and mentor highly technical teams 
  * Leadership grounded in collaboration, rigor, and accountability 
  * Clear communicator across hardware, software, and research organizations 
  * Comfortable operating in fast-evolving technical environments 
  * Strong technical judgment and prioritization capability 
  * Ability to reconcile performance objectives with scalability and operational constraints 
  * Commitment to advancing scalable quantum computing technologies


Nord Quantique is committed to the principles of equity, diversity, and inclusion in its training, workplaces, and activities. We encourage applications from diverse backgrounds and traditionally marginalized groups.

We are seeking a highly motivated experimental physicist or engineer with experience in quantum systems, cryogenic experiments, and large-scale validation environments. Experience with superconducting quantum systems is an asset.
The mission of this role is to execute and develop validation methodologies that characterize system-level performance, operational robustness, and long-term reliability of integrated quantum computing systems under realistic scaling constraints.

  * Analyze experimental data and communicate findings to cross-functional engineering and research teams
  * Support development of deployment-readiness criteria, acceptance metrics, and performance-gating methodologies

  * Relevant experience in quantum technologies, superconducting quantum hardware, or complex cryogenic experimental systems
  * Experience executing experimental campaigns in multidisciplinary environments


**Sought expertise and knowledge:**
  * Circuit QED and superconducting quantum systems
  * Cryogenic experimental systems
  * RF, microwave, and pulsed measurements of superconducting quantum systems
  * Experimental automation and calibration workflows
  * Data analysis and statistical interpretation
  * System-level validation and performance characterization
  * Reliability engineering or long-duration stability testing, an asset
  * Python programming and version control best practices, an asset
  * Advanced level in English, to communicate with international team members
  * Intermediate knowledge of French, an asset


**Sought profile:**
  * Strong engineering mindset
  * Strong experimental rigor and analytical thinking
  * Systems-thinking mindset
  * Team-oriented and collaborative
  * Adaptable to evolving technical priorities
  * Clear and concise technical communicator
  * Comfortable operating in highly interdisciplinary environments
  * Motivated by solving long-term scalability and reliability challenges in quantum computing

The Quantum Hardware Department operates as the end user at the heart of Nord Quantique, responsible for delivering improved quantum processor performance integrated into larger systems. The mission of the Quantum Control & Calibration team is to develop and refine high-fidelity control primitives and calibration strategies that minimize error rates and enable next-generation logical performance.
More specifically, this individual will start, grow, and lead this team of quantum hardware physicists to deliver quantum processor performance improvements propelling Nord Quantique along our ambitious technical R&D roadmap. They will lead roadmap thrusts to push our hardware capabilities and continuously deepen our understanding of the science behind it. The person will develop new methods and hardware through a strong interaction with our theory, design and experimental teams.

  * Lead and support the day-to-day work of Quantum Control & Calibration Team
  * Define, lead, and manage experimental campaigns to deliver roadmap thrusts
  * Support the team’s growth through mentoring, recruitment, and skill development
  * Own primitive gate schemes and calibration standards to improve performance
  * Digest scientific data and feedback to the EM Design and Quantum Theory teams
  * Represent Nord Quantique externally at scientific conferences

  * Minimum 5 years experience in quantum computing or superconducting circuit research
  * Experience leading or coordinating experimental efforts in academic or industrial setting
  * Strong background in interdisciplinary collaboration and research-to-development transfer


We recognize that expertise can come from a variety of academic or professional paths—not all of which follow conventional trajectories—and we value diversity in that regard.

  * Led and delivered experimental campaigns
  * Circuit QED
  * Practical understanding of quantum error correction 
  * Team management, priority setting and efficient project execution
  * Bosonic codes, an asset
  * Familiarity with designing superconducting circuits, an asset
  * Quantum gate calibration and benchmarking, an asset
  * Advanced level in English, to communicate with international team members
  * Intermediate knowledge of French, an asset


**Sought profile:**

  * Leadership grounded in empathy and respect for individual growth
  * Strong technical vision grounded in scientific rigor
  * Clear and inclusive communication across diverse technical teams

Working in close collaboration with experts at Nord Quantique, the chosen candidate will:
  * Perform electromagnetic simulations to test and optimize quantum processor designs.
  * Develop and refine EM structures to enhance coherence properties and coupling rates.
  * Collaborate on the development of computational tools for design automation.
  * Work with cross-functional teams to integrate optimized electromagnetic components.

  * Proficiency in electromagnetic simulation software such as Ansys HFSS, PALACE, or similar.

  * Familiarity with automation and high-performance computing techniques.
  * Advanced level in English, to communicate with international team members

  * Adaptability in a fast-paced research environment with a proactive and innovative mindset.

**Our current needs**
As we scale up our quantum processors and supporting infrastructure, we are seeking a skilled and self-driven Mechanical Designer to join our Mechanical Design and Manufacturing Team. This role focuses on the CAD of the cryogenic dilution refrigerators setups, precision design of 3D qubit assemblies and structural and thermal analysis of these systems.
**Key responsibilities:**
  * Develop CAD of 3D cavity qubits and GD&T drawings, ensuring mechanical precision and manufacturability.
  * Explore mechanical elements of designs and manufacturing techniques in cavity designs to improve the repeatability of assembly.
  * Design and develop assembly procedures and fixtures for precise assembly of nanofabricated chips.
  * Design and document the cryogenic setups for dilution refrigerator systems.
  * Conduct structural and thermal analysis on the cryogenic setup to support the payload and thermal performance, analytically and numerically.
  * Support the organization of CAD libraries, revision control systems, and documentation to support long-term system scaling and reproducibility.
  * Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (Design, Quantum Hardware, and Nanofabrication) to iterate on designs.
  * Coordinate with external vendors, suppliers, and development partners to support machining, sourcing, and integration activities.


This is a dynamic role within a fast-paced, research-driven environment, offering the opportunity to contribute directly to the advancement of scalable quantum hardware.
**Education and experience requirements:**
  * Bachelor’s degree (BSc) or Master’s degree (MSc) in Mechanical Engineering or in related field with minimum 2 years of mechanical design experience.
  * Must have R&D industry experience, preferably within scientific instrumentation, cryogenic, space instrumentation, semiconductor industries, with a proven track record in interdisciplinary teams.  
  



**Sought expertise and knowledge:**
  * Experience in CAD modeling and assembly design for precision mechanical systems, using Solidworks, Onshape or other advanced CAD software
  * Demonstrated ability to optimize designs for repeatability required for scaling
  * Experience with revision control and documentation of CAD
  * Familiarity with Design for Manufacturing and Assembly (DFM/DFA) principles
  * Excellent ability to deliver results within tight deadlines
  * Experience with stress and thermal analysis using FEA simulation software
  * Advanced level in English, to communicate with team members
  * Intermediate knowledge of French an asset

  * **Excellent organizational and prioritization skills** focused on achieving company objectives

  * Strong attention to detail and commitment to process rigour and execution quality.

				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p>At Nord Quantique we’re working to change the world through quantum computing, and that means working in a highly dynamic environment at the forefront of quantum technology development, at the heart of Sherbrooke’s vibrant Quantum Innovation Zone ecosystem. Our interdisciplinary team is laser-focused on solving grand challenges and making quantum computing a reality. </p><p>Nord Quantique is an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive work environment where all individuals can fulfill their aspirations while being supported and heard. This also means that we offer flexible work schedule and adapt our methods for the wellbeing of every individual. </p>								</div>

				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p>Nord Quantique offers extensive moving support, including help with immigration procedures, finding a suitable home, access to childcare and schools, even helping your partner to find a new job. We offer a relocation compensation package and competitive salaries. </p>								</div>

				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p><b>Quantum System Validation Team Lead</b><b><br></b><span style="font-weight: 400;">(permanent position, 40 hours per week)</span><span style="font-weight: 400;"><br></span><b>Contact: </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">(819) 975-4654</span><b>, </b><a href="mailto:career@nordquantique.ca"><span style="font-weight: 400;">career@nordquantique.ca</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span></p><p><b>About </b><a href="http://www.nordquantique.ca/"><b>Nord Quantique</b></a></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Nord Quantique, we strive to build impactful technology by relentlessly pushing our understanding of quantum science. We are an interdisciplinary team of scientists, engineers, innovators, builders, and enthusiasts on a mission to make quantum computing a reality. Our quantum processors are designed to reduce errors in quantum computation to facilitate long-term scaling toward fully-fledged quantum computers. We believe it is our responsibility to deliver this transformative technology for the greater good of society.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Located at the heart of the beautiful Eastern Townships region in the province of Quebec, Canada, we develop our technology in the vibrant quantum ecosystem of Sherbrooke. Leaning on more than a decade of vertical integration of quantum hardware, we are leveraging a comprehensive pool of quantum technology and micro-electronic infrastructure to deploy our quantum computing technology.</span></p><p><b>Our current needs</b></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We are seeking a senior technical leader with strong expertise in engineering of quantum systems with an emphasis on large-scale experimental validation and reliability engineering. Beyond technical excellence, we are looking for someone capable of building and leading a multidisciplinary validation organization operating at the interface between R&D and large-scale system deployment.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The mission of the Quantum System Validation team is to validate preservation of multimode bosonic logical performance under scaling-induced physical variability and emergent error channels, and to enforce the reliability standards required for deployment-ready quantum computing systems.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">More specifically, this individual will define and grow Nord Quantique’s system-level validation strategy, establish reliability engineering discipline across increasingly complex quantum computing systems, and lead the development of scalable validation methodologies supporting future utility-scale architectures.</span></p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The role combines quantum system validation, operational robustness engineering, and long-duration reliability qualification of a scaled and integrated system covering cryogenic, control, and software.</span></p><p><b>Keys responsibilities:</b></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Define and lead the system-level validation and reliability strategy across successive quantum computer generations</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Build and support a multidisciplinary validation organization spanning quantum validation, operational robustness, and reliability engineering</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Define deployment-readiness criteria, quantitative system acceptance metrics, performance gates, and validation methodologies supporting deployment decisions</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Lead long-duration stability testing and drift characterization campaigns</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Establish FMEA methodologies, qualification procedures, and reliability governance frameworks</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Validate preservation of logical performance under scaling-induced physical variability and emergent error channels</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Oversee validation of hardware–software orchestration, including calibration workflows, boot-up sequencing, and software integration</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Coordinate cross-functional root-cause analysis activities during system bring-up and validation failures</span></li></ul><p><b>Education and experience requirements:</b></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">PhD in experimental physics, engineering, quantum computing, systems engineering, or a related field</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">+5 years of postgraduate experience in quantum technologies, advanced hardware systems, or complex cryogenic system development</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Including 2 years of relevant industry experience</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Demonstrated experience leading large-scale experimental or system validation efforts</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Experience building or managing multidisciplinary technical teams</span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Strong experience operating at the interface between research and engineering environments</span></li></ul><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">We recognize that expertise can come from a variety of academic or professional paths—not all of which follow conventional trajectories—and we value diversity in that regard.</span></p><p><b>Sought expertise and knowledge:</b></p><ul><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Superconducting quantum computing systems </span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">System-level validation and performance qualification </span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Scaling-induced noise and drift mechanisms </span></li><li style="font-weight: 400;" 									<p><b>Senior Electromagnetic Designer</b></p>
<p>Permanent position, 40 hours per week</p>
<p><b>Contact</b>: (819)975-4654, <a href="mailto:career@nordquantique.ca" target="_blank" rel="noopener">career@nordquantique.ca</a><br><b>Supervisor</b>: Design Team Lead<br><b>Subordinate</b>: None<br><br></p>
<p><b>About Nord Quantique</b></p>
<p>At Nord Quantique, we strive to build impactful technology by relentlessly pushing our understanding of quantum science. We are an interdisciplinary team of scientists, engineers, innovators, builders, and enthusiasts on a mission to make quantum computing a reality. Our quantum processors are designed to reduce errors in quantum computation to facilitate long-term scaling toward fully-fledged quantum computers. We believe it is our responsibility to deliver this transformative technology for the greater good of society.</p>
<p>Located at the heart of the beautiful Eastern Townships region in the province of Quebec, Canada, we develop our technology in the vibrant quantum ecosystem of Sherbrooke. Leaning on more than a decade of vertical integration of quantum hardware, we are leveraging a comprehensive pool of quantum technology and micro-electronic infrastructure to deploy our quantum computing technology.</p>
<p><b><br>Our current needs</b></p>
<p>We are scaling our activities alongside our quantum processors. Our team thrives on creativity and innovation, constantly pushing the boundaries of what is possible in quantum hardware design. We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Electromagnetic Designer to join our Design team and contribute to the development of the next generation of high-performance quantum processors.</p>
<p>Working in close collaboration with experts at Nord Quantique, the chosen candidate will:</p>
<ul>
<li>Perform electromagnetic simulations to test and optimize quantum processor designs.</li>
<li>Develop and refine EM structures to enhance coherence properties and coupling rates.</li>
<li>Collaborate on the development of computational tools for design automation.</li>
<li>Work with cross-functional teams to integrate optimized electromagnetic components.</li>
<li>Conduct design validation and performance analysis to improve hardware efficiency.<br><br></li>
</ul>
<p>The selected candidate will operate within a highly dynamic environment and will be an essential member of the Design team, directly contributing to advancing our technology at its physical core.<br></p><p><b>Education and experience requirements</b>:</p>
<ul>
<li>PhD or equivalent industry experience in electrical engineering, physics, or a related field.</li>
<li>5+ years of experience in electromagnetic design and finite-element simulations, an asset</li>
<li>Experience in quantum hardware design and superconducting microwave circuits.</li>
<li>Industry experience with a proven track record in interdisciplinary R&D teams.</li>
</ul>
<p><b><br>Sought expertise and knowledge:</b></p>
<ul>
<li>Deep understanding of electromagnetic theory and microwave engineering principles.</li>
<li>Proficiency in electromagnetic simulation software such as Ansys HFSS, PALACE, or similar.</li>
<li>Experience in computational modeling and Python programming for scientific applications.</li>
<li>Understanding of loss mechanisms and material properties in superconducting devices.</li>
<li>Familiarity with automation and high-performance computing techniques.</li>
<li>Advanced level in English, to communicate with international team members</li>
<li>Intermediate knowledge of French, an asset<br><br></li>
</ul>
<p><b>Sought profile:</b></p>
<ul>
<li>Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills to drive design optimizations.</li>
<li>Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with cross-functional teams.</li>
<li>Ability to manage multiple projects and prioritize complex design challenges.</li>
<li>Adaptability in a fast-paced research environment with a proactive and innovative mindset.</li>
<li>Passion for advancing quantum computing and deep-tech. </li>
</ul>
<p><b> </b></p>
<p><b>Working at Nord Quantique</b></p>
<p>At Nord Quantique you will be part of something special: working in a highly dynamic environment at the forefront of quantum technology development, while benefiting from a vibrant ecosystem located at the heart of the Quantum Innovation Zone of the picturesque city of Sherbrooke. Our interdisciplinary team is laser-focused on solving grand challenges and making quantum computing a reality.</p>
<p>Nord Quantique is an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive work environment where all individuals can fulfill their aspirations while being supported and heard. This also means that we offer flexible work schedule and adapt our methods for the wellbeing of every individual.</p>
<p><b>Moving to Nord Quantique</b></p>
<p>Nord Quantique offers extensive moving support, including help with immigration paperwork, with finding a place to live, with access to childcare and schools, even helping your partner to find a new job. We offer a relocation compensation package and competitive salaries.<br><br></p>
<p><b>Salary</b>: $132 000 – $150 000<br><b>Benefits</b>: Group insurance (including dental care), virtual care, lifestyle spending account (all available after 3 months of service)<br><b>Vacation</b>: 5 weeks/year<br><b>Working language</b>: English and/or French<br><b>Workplace</b>: Espace Quantique 1, 1950 rue Roy, Sherbrooke, QC, J1K 1B7</p><p><br></p>								</div>

				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-c719b85 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="c719b85" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p class="p2"><b>Recruiter <br></b>(permanent position, 40 hour per week) <br><b>Contact: (819) 975-4654, career@nordquantique.ca </b><b></b></p><p class="p2"><b> </b></p><p class="p2"><b>Supervisor: Director of HR<br></b><b>Subordinate: –</b></p><p class="p2"><b> </b></p><p class="p2"><b>About Nord Quantique </b><b></b></p><p class="p2">At Nord Quantique, we strive to build impactful technology by relentlessly pushing our understanding of quantum science. We are an interdisciplinary team of scientists, engineers, innovators, builders, and enthusiasts on a mission to make quantum computing a reality. Our quantum processors are designed to reduce errors in quantum computation to facilitate long-term scaling toward fully-fledged quantum computers. We believe it is our responsibility to deliver this transformative technology for the greater good of society.</p><p class="p2">Located at the heart of the beautiful Eastern Townships region in the province of Quebec, Canada, we develop our technology in the vibrant quantum ecosystem of Sherbrooke. Leaning on more than a decade of vertical integration of quantum hardware, we are leveraging a comprehensive pool of quantum technology and micro-electronic infrastructure to deploy our quantum computing technology.</p><p class="p2"><b> </b></p><p class="p2"><b>Our needs </b><b></b></p><p class="p2">As Nord Quantique continues to grow, we are strengthening our talent acquisition capabilities to support the hiring of highly specialized talent across scientific, engineering, and operational domains. Delivering a consistent and high-quality hiring experience is a key component of building a successful team and supporting the company’s long-term objectives.</p><p class="p2">We are looking for a Recruiter to play a central role in executing Nord Quantique’s growth activities. This role will contribute to ensuring that hiring processes are structured, efficient, and aligned with Nord Quantique’s standards, while delivering an engaging candidate experience.</p><p class="p2">Working closely with the Head of Talent Acquisition and HR team, the Recruiter will support the execution of recruitment processes and progressively contribute to sourcing and pipeline development, helping the organization attract and integrate diverse talent. This will be realized by building an engaging candidate experience and acting as a key point of interaction between candidates and the organization.</p><p class="p2"><b> </b></p><p class="p2"><b>The role </b></p><p class="p2">The Recruiter will support the execution and continuous improvement of Nord Quantique’s hiring processes. Working in close collaboration with the Head of talent acquisition and HR team, the candidate will:</p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li2">Manage the end-to-end coordination of recruitment processes, including interview scheduling, candidate follow-ups, and process tracking.</li></ul><ul class="ul1"><li class="li2">Ensure a high-quality and consistent candidate experience throughout all stages of the hiring process.</li><li class="li2">Act as the primary point of contact for candidates throughout the recruitment process and ensure clear and timely communication with candidates and hiring teams</li><li class="li2">Support hiring and technical teams by ensuring that interview processes are structured, documented, and seamlessly tracked.</li><li class="li2">Maintain accurate and up-to-date candidate data within the ATS and recruitment tools.</li><li class="li2">Contribute to the development and improvement of recruitment processes and documentation.</li><li class="li2">Support sourcing activities, with an increasing focus on outbound sourcing and pipeline development over time.</li><li class="li2">Participate in recruitment events and initiatives, in collaboration with the talent acquisition team and Marketing when required.</li><li class="li3">Act as a key operational partner to reduce the recruitment load on technical teams.</li></ul><p class="p3"><b> </b></p><p class="p3"><b><br>Education and experience requirements: </b><b></b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li4">Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology, science, engineering, or any related field.</li><li class="li4">3–6 years of experience in recruitment, preferably in technical or technology-driven environments.</li><li class="li4">Experience supporting or managing recruitment processes in a structured and fast-paced environment.</li><li class="li4">Experience working with Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) and recruitment tools.</li><li class="li4">Experience recruiting for technical roles is strongly preferred. Quantum computing recruitment experience is an asset.</li><li class="li3">Experience in deep-tech or research-driven environments is an asset.</li></ul><p class="p3"><b> </b></p><p class="p3"><b><br>Sought expertise and knowledge: </b><b></b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li4">Strong understanding of recruitment processes and candidate lifecycle management.</li><li class="li4">Familiarity with technical recruiting environments and the ability to understand hiring needs across engineering, research and support roles.</li><li class="li4">Knowledge of sourcing techniques, including outbound sourcing methods.</li><li class="li4">Ability to manage and maintain accurate recruitment data and reporting tools.</li><li class="li4">Understanding of structured interview processes and evaluation frameworks.</li><li class="li4">Familiarity with ATS platforms and recruitment tools.</li><li class="li3">Bilingual English/French is preferred.</li></ul><p class="p1"><b> </b></p><p class="p1"><b><br>Sought profile: </b><b></b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li2">Ability to communicate effectively with candidates and hiring managers in a technical environment</li><li class="li2">Strong attention to detail and commitment to process rigour and execution quality.</li><li class="li2">Ability to deliver a high-quality and engaging candidate experience, reflecting Nord Quantique’s standards and culture.</li><li class="li2">Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust with candidates and interact effectively with diverse stakeholders.</li><li class="li2">Ability to clearly and effectively communicate complex or technical roles to candidates.</li><li class="li2">Strong organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple recruitment processes simultaneously.</li><li class="li2">Proactive and reliable, with a focus on reducing operational load for other teams.</li><li class="li2">Ability to operate in a fast-growing and evolving organization.</li><li class="li1">Strong alignment with Nord Quantique’s mission and collaborative culture.</li></ul><p class="p1"> </p><p class="p1">Nord Quantique is committed to the principles of equity, diversity, and inclusion in its training, workplaces, and activities. We encourage applications from diverse backgrounds and traditionally marginalized groups.</p><p class="p1"><b> </b></p><p class="p1"><b>Working at Nord Quantique </b><b></b></p><p class="p1">At Nord Quantique, you will be part of something special: working in a highly dynamic environment at the forefront of quantum technology development while benefiting from a vibrant ecosystem located at the heart of the Quantum Innovation Zone of the picturesque city of Sherbrooke. Our interdisciplinary team is laser-focused on solving grand challenges and making quantum computing a reality.</p><p class="p1">Nord Quantique is an equal-opportunity employer, and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive work environment where all individuals can fulfill their aspirations while being supported and heard. This also means that we offer flexible work schedules and adapt our methods for the well-being of every individual.</p><p class="p1"><b> </b></p><p class="p1"><b>Moving to Nord Quantique </b><b></b></p><p class="p1">Nord Quantique offers extensive moving support, including help with immigration paperwork, with finding a place to live, with access to childcare and schools, even helping your partner to find a new job. We offer a relocation compensation package and competitive salaries.</p><p class="p1"><b> </b></p><p class="p1"><b>Salary: $81,500 – $95,000 </b><b></b></p><p class="p1"><b>Benefits: Group insurance (including dental care), virtual care, lifestyle spending account (all available </b>after 3 months of service)</p><p class="p1"><b>Working language: English and/or French (bilingualism is an asset) </b><b></b></p><p class="p1"><b>Workplace</b>: Espace Quantique 1, 1950 rue Roy, Sherbrooke, QC, J1K 1B7</p>								</div>

				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-6c4aa22 elementor-widget elementor-widget-text-editor" data-id="6c4aa22" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="text-editor.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
									<p class="p2"><b>Data integrator <br></b>(permanent position, 40 hours per week) <br><b><br>Contact: (819) 975-4654, career@nordquantique.ca </b><b></b></p><p class="p2"><b><br>Team:</b> Industrialization & Operations <br><b>Supervisor: </b>Director of Operations<b> <br>Subordinate: </b>None</p><p class="p2"><b><br>About Nord Quantique </b><b></b></p><p class="p2">At Nord Quantique, we strive to build impactful technology by relentlessly pushing our understanding of quantum science. We are an interdisciplinary team of scientists, engineers, innovators, builders, and enthusiasts on a mission to make quantum computing a reality. Our quantum processors are designed to reduce errors in quantum computation to facilitate long-term scaling toward fully-fledged quantum computers. We believe it is our responsibility to deliver this transformative technology for the greater good of society.</p><p class="p2">Located at the heart of the beautiful Eastern Townships region in the province of Quebec, Canada, we develop our technology in the vibrant quantum ecosystem of Sherbrooke. Leaning on more than a decade of vertical integration of quantum hardware, we are leveraging a comprehensive pool of quantum technology and micro-electronic infrastructure to deploy our quantum computing technology.</p><p class="p2"><b><br>Our current needs</b></p><p class="p2">As Nord Quantique continues to grow, the Industrialization and Operations team is building the structure and discipline needed to support that growth. The team works at the intersection of engineering and operations, ensuring that processes are robust, tools are reliable, and decisions are grounded in clear operational data.</p><p class="p2">Data integrator will play a central role in bringing order and clarity to our data landscape. This person will enable the data access from various source into a single place of analysis and as well the development of reliable industrial dashboards. By structuring data across PLM, MES, SharePoint sites, databases, procurement tools, and future systems, they will ensure that operational metrics are consistent, traceable, and trustworthy — so leaders can rely on them to guide real decisions.</p><p class="p2"><b><br>Working in close collaboration with all technical team leaders, the candidate will: </b><b></b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li3">Design and operate a data access model.</li><li class="li2">Build and maintain connectors/pipelines to consolidate data from multiple platforms (e.g., MES/ERP/PLM, lab systems, ticketing, spreadsheets, databases, cloud storage).</li></ul><ul class="ul1"><li class="li2">Develop and maintain industrial dashboards (production / yield / quality / procurement / inventory / tool uptime) with clear definitions and traceable metrics.</li><li class="li2">Support security and compliance needs (vendor assessments for data tools, audit readiness, risk reviews, cybersecurity event response procedure) as applicable.</li><li class="li3">Create lightweight training and enablement (how-to guides, governance playbooks, onboarding for dashboard users and data owners).</li></ul><p class="p3"><b><br>Education and experience requirements : </b><b></b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li4">Bachelor’s degree in Software Engineering, or in a related field, or equivalent experience.</li><li class="li4">Minimum 5+ years of experience in data governance, data engineering, analytics engineering, data platform engineering, and/or security governance in an operational environment.</li><li class="li4">Experience integrating manufacturing/industrial systems (MES/PLM/ERP) and operational reporting, an asset.</li><li class="li3">Experience supporting security/compliance programs (SOC2 Type 2, ISO2700-1), an asset.</li></ul><p class="p3"><b><br>Sought expertise and knowledge: </b><b></b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li2">Ability to define controls and coordinate implementation with IT</li><li class="li2">Building/operating integrations and pipelines across heterogeneous systems (structured + semi-structured sources)</li><li class="li2">Data quality monitoring and operationalization (checks, monitoring, issue workflows)</li><li class="li2">Power BI (preferred) and/or comparable BI tools; dashboard design for operations (drill-down, consistency, governance); scientific visualisation software (e.g. JMP)</li><li class="li2">Advanced level in English, to communicate with international team members</li><li class="li3">Intermediate knowledge of French, an asset</li></ul><p class="p3"><b><br>Sought profile:</b></p><ul class="ul1"><li class="li6">Pragmatism and good judgment (security vs. usability trade-offs)</li><li class="li6">Ownership mindset and ability to drive adoption</li><li class="li6">Clear documentation and communication</li><li class="li6">Comfort operating in ambiguity and proposing workable target architectures</li><li class="li6">Proactive approach to problem solving</li><li class="li6">Comfortable managing multiple projects and aligning execution with evolving priorities</li><li class="li6">Diplomatic approach to working with external partners</li><li class="li6">Highly organized, meticulous and efficient with a process-orientated mindset</li><li class="li3">Commitment to advancing quantum computing and building meaningful technology</li></ul><div> </div><div><p class="p1">Nord Quantique is committed to the principles of equity, diversity, and inclusion in its training, workplaces, and activities. We encourage applications from diverse backgrounds and traditionally marginalized groups.</p><p class="p1"><b><br>Working at Nord Quantique </b><b></b></p><p class="p1">At Nord Quantique, you will be part of something special: working in a highly dynamic environment at the forefront of quantum technology development while benefiting from a vibrant ecosystem located at the heart of the Quantum Innovation Zone of the picturesque city of Sherbrooke. Our interdisciplinary team is laser-focused on solving grand challenges and making quantum computing a reality.</p><p class="p1">Nord Quantique is an equal-opportunity employer, and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive work environment where all individuals can fulfill their aspirations while being supported and heard. This also means that we offer flexible work schedules and adapt our methods for the well-being of every individual.</p><p class="p1"><b><br>Moving to Nord Quantique </b><b></b></p><p class="p1">Nord Quantique offers extensive moving support, including help with immigration paperwork, with finding a place to live, with access to childcare and schools, even helping your partner to find a new job. We offer a relocation compensation package and competitive salaries.</p><p class="p1"><b><br>Salary: $114,000 – </b>$127,000<br><b>Vacation: </b>5 weeks per year<br><b>Benefits: </b>Group insurance (including dental care), virtual care, lifestyle spending account (all available after 3 months of service)<br><b>Working language: </b>English and French<br><b>Workplace: </b>Espace Quantique 1, 1950 rue Roy, Sherbrooke, QC, J1K 1B7<b><br></b></p></div>								</div>

									<p><b>General Application</b></p><p><b>Our current needs</b></p>
<p>We are scaling our activities alongside our quantum processors. In this effort we are looking for an exceptional individual with a strong track record of experimental work in the field of quantum computing with superconducting circuits. This person will assist our team in the development and scaling up of our technology.</p>
<p>More specifically, this individual will lead experimental campaigns to push our hardware capabilities and continuously deepen our understanding of the science behind it. The person will develop new methods and hardware through a strong interaction with our theory, design and experimental teams.</p>
<p><b> </b></p>
<p><b>Sought expertise</b></p>
<ul>
<li>PhD experience in working in experimental physics with superconducting circuits</li>
<li>Proven experience in leading experimental campaigns and sub-teams</li>
<li>Proven ability to design, characterize and push the performances of superconducting circuits</li>
<li>Deep knowledge of experimental techniques related to the characterization of superconducting circuits including qubit spectroscopy and state-of-the-art time-domain techniques</li>
<li>Good understanding of the theoretical concepts behind superconducting circuits</li>
<li>Ability to program experiments in python-based environments</li>
</ul>								</div>

		<script type="text/javascript">

			jQuery(document).ready(function(){
					
				function ucBackgroundOverlayPutStart(){
				
					var objBG = jQuery(".unlimited-elements-background-overlay").not(".uc-bg-attached");

					if(objBG.length == 0)
						return(false);

					objBG.each(function(index, bgElement){

						var objBgElement = jQuery(bgElement);

						var targetID = objBgElement.data("forid");

						var location = objBgElement.data("location");

						switch(location){
							case "body":
							case "body_front":
								var objTarget = jQuery("body");
							break;
							case "layout":
							case "layout_front":
								var objLayout = jQuery("*[data-id=\""+targetID+"\"]");
								var objTarget = objLayout.parents(".elementor");
								if(objTarget.length > 1)
									objTarget = jQuery(objTarget[0]);
							break;
							default:
								var objTarget = jQuery("*[data-id=\""+targetID+"\"]");
							break;
						}


						if(objTarget.length == 0)
							return(true);

						var objVideoContainer = objTarget.children(".elementor-background-video-container");

						if(objVideoContainer.length == 1)
							objBgElement.detach().insertAfter(objVideoContainer).show();
						else
							objBgElement.detach().prependTo(objTarget).show();


						var objTemplate = objBgElement.children("template");

						if(objTemplate.length){
							
					        var clonedContent = objTemplate[0].content.cloneNode(true);

					    	var objScripts = jQuery(clonedContent).find("script");
					    	if(objScripts.length)
					    		objScripts.attr("type","text/javascript");
					        
					        objBgElement.append(clonedContent);
							
							objTemplate.remove();
						}

						objBgElement.trigger("bg_attached");
						objBgElement.addClass("uc-bg-attached");

					});
				}

				ucBackgroundOverlayPutStart();

				jQuery( document ).on( 'elementor/popup/show', ucBackgroundOverlayPutStart);
				jQuery( "body" ).on( 'uc_dom_updated', ucBackgroundOverlayPutStart);

			});


		</script>
